Mosquitoes

Explore the drawers of London's Natural History Museum

London’s Natural History Museum has a collection of mosquitoes going back to the 1880s. These animals are important to public health, since they could be used to study the history of vector-borne diseases.

Natural History Museum (2014). Collection specimens [Data set]. Natural History Museum. https://doi.org/10.5519/qd.qv131qog

Loading Culicidae specimens...

Unable to load specimens

There was an error loading the Culicidae specimens.

NHM API temporarily unavailable - showing example Culicidae specimens
← Back to Octopus Observations Next: Mars Weather Station →

Background

Resilience Continuum is a project by Claire Nyquist which explores science through data visualization. I'm excited about the combination of the apparently disparate fields of design and public health as they come together in data visualization.
